name: cloud-delegation
description: Linear-as-queue + cloud-agent (Codex, Cursor, Sprite) delegation workflow — issue creation, polling, push-back-vs-fix matrix, agent env constraints, AGENTS.md auto-sync hook. v1.11: add `sprite-claude-code` skill — operational reference for Fly Sprite-hosted Claude Code as a third cloud-delegation target alongside Codex Cloud and Cursor Background. Covers the rc43 CLI surface (sprite create/exec/sessions/checkpoint/info/api), three auth threads (interactive OAuth, `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` + `claude --bare`), reachability profile (full hex.pm, full PATH, Elixir/Erlang/Mix pre-installed at `/.sprite/bin` without asdf, Tidewave + live Phoenix reachable in same VM, public HTTPS URL per sprite), sleep/wake characteristics (30s idle, ext4 + JuiceFS + Litestream-backed persistence, ~0.4s wake-on-exec), cost ceiling (`claude --max-budget-usd` + checkpoint/restore + `sprite destroy`), known orchestration gaps (no built-in completion signal, `claude --print` exit code unreliable, no Linear-poll wrapper yet). Adds a 'Fly Sprite (third target)' cross-reference paragraph to `cloud-agent-environments.md`. v1.10: auto-merge widened from cloud-agent-only to all feature-branch PRs (worktree branches qualify; only PRs whose head IS the repo's default branch are out of scope) — autonomy-first stance ('PRs + audits are the inspection surface'); `[BLOCK-MERGE]` label is now the universal per-PR manual override (cloud-agent or self-authored). Removes the old `Self-authored PRs forbidden` line and the matching ROADMAP follow-up. linear-workflow gains a `Self-Authored Worktree Flow` section with the per-phase Linear-comment cadence (pickup, PR open, verdict, merge, audit) for non-delegated worktree work; the five-rule asymmetry table simplified accordingly. v1.9.2: linear-workflow generalized hardcoded `main` references to `the repo's default branch (main / master / development)` across merge-train bookkeeping, code-only PR scoping, audit-review chain wording, bundled-revisions variant, and the no-Linear fallback section; new Recovery-if-interrupted paragraph for the bundled-revisions variant covering staged-but-uncommitted edits left on the default branch when audit-review aborts mid-run. v1.9.1: Tidewave-on-Cursor reach verified (2026-05-07) — `curl localhost:<port>/tidewave/mcp` always works; native `CallMcpTool` requires pre-session start. Push-back-vs-fix matrix updated: live-data/runtime-state tasks no longer Cursor-eligibility-blocker. v1.9: linear-workflow skill restructure — consolidate Codex/Cursor delegation flows, add explicit Status Transitions section, rename + clarify Code-Only PRs + Required Acceptance Criteria, drop redundant marker/MCP-tool subsections, scope note distinguishing Linear's first-party @Linear agent from third-party cloud agents. Substantial slimming (~530 lines removed, ~450 added) without behavior changes. v1.8: capture cartouche batching learnings — eligibility filter order (Codex-suspended → [CSR], per-agent env constraints, sibling-repo blockers), batch sizing rules (2+1+1 splits, pause-for-/compact, bundle-only-on-shared-module-tier-shape, parallelism gated on file-scope-non-overlap), honest-gap discipline (4 paths when queue drains to zero — wait/pivot LOCAL/cross-repo/review-mode — never silently backfill from non-eligible tasks). v1.7: add bundled-code-revisions bookkeeping variant — when a cloud-agent PR is mostly-good but ships small dead/unwanted code AND the agent's harness can't self-validate the fix (Cursor dialyzer OOM, Codex no-hex.pm, no-Tidewave anywhere), reviewer merges-as-is and bundles code revisions into the canonical commit-review Step 14–16 post-merge bookkeeping commit (single commit naming PR #N + INE-M for traceability), trading evaluator-separation for round-trip-cost savings. Documents preserved/lost trade-offs vs. canonical doc-only flow + four when-NOT-to-use guards. v1.6: retire don't-push hedge in favor of merge-train mode (`flow-review`) — workflow-only batch processor for 2+ open cloud-agent PRs that classifies by tier, dependency-sorts the queue, and runs the rebase cascade after each user-confirmed merge under the new mechanical-only rebase carve-out in delegation-rules.md. Also adds pre-flight conflict detection on N≥2 batch issue creation (touch-matrix on `## Files to modify` blocks → bundle / serializer / local / parallel recommendation), preventing cartouche-style fan-out where 4 of 9 issues touched the same registry file.
